hero.subtitle: Brazilian Educational and Cultural Centre — Preserving Brazilian language and culture in the United Kingdom since 1997.

about.title: About BREACC
about.description: BREACC is a registered charity in England (no. 1087726) dedicated to Brazilian education and culture for children and young people.
about.teaching_desc: Weekly classes that develop reading, writing and communication in Portuguese.
about.community_desc: A welcoming space for Brazilian and Lusophone families in the United Kingdom.
about.culture_desc: Celebrations, music, dance and traditions that keep Brazilian culture alive
about.identity_desc: We help children develop a strong and proud bicultural identity.

events.title: Upcoming Events
events.subtitle: Keep up with our upcoming events and come along!
events.festa_junina_desc: Our traditional Festa Junina with typical food, quadrilha dancing and lots of fun!

groups.title: Learning Stages
groups.subtitle: Six distinct stages that develop social, motor and linguistic skills in varied ways.
groups.jardim_desc: Children aged 2 to 3 develop socialisation and interaction skills in Portuguese through songs, games and folk traditions. This is a special group as parents actively participate in the activities, passing on their experiences, history and heritage in a playful and enjoyable way. In this way, children learn to love our culture from an early age, as a starting point for learning Portuguese as a Heritage Language (POLH).
groups.cante_desc: Children aged 4 to 5 learn traditional Brazilian songs and games. Teaching is developed in a playful way, without a focus on spelling, given that literacy is a unique process and children are already being or will be taught to read and write in British primary schools. With plenty of interaction, children are encouraged to work independently and in groups.
groups.pantanal_desc: For children aged 6 to 7, the Pantanal group offers the opportunity to develop oral, writing and comprehension skills through games and play. With diverse research and project activities, students are encouraged to develop their reading and writing abilities.
groups.sertao_desc: Children aged 8 to 9 are encouraged to deepen their oral and writing language skills in the Sertão group. With diverse teaching methods, always using play as a starting point, these students progress in their communication skills, always using the Portuguese language as a guiding thread.
groups.amazonia_desc: From 10 to 12 years old, this group expands children's oral communication skills and focuses on various aspects of expression in Portuguese. Children are exposed to songs, games and texts that are gradually more complex and begin to develop a greater appreciation for using the language. Group activities aim to encourage interaction between children and an effective exchange of experiences.
groups.cerrado_desc: This group uses educational activities to consolidate grammatical, literary, playful and numerical skills based on the needs of young people. With teenagers from 13 years of age, this group offers great support to those who wish to prepare for the General Certificate of Secondary Education (GCSE) in Portuguese. Students are motivated to write in a more academic way, expressing their opinions and reinterpreting literary and artistic works.

testimonials.title: Testimonials
testimonials.subtitle: What families say about BREACC
testimonials.t1: We are eternally grateful for the work that BREACC does with our children. Our daughter learnt to read and write at BREACC. It is an emotional and cultural lifeline for us parents.
testimonials.t2: A brilliant school whose aim is to pass on our culture and language to children in a playful and engaging way!
testimonials.t3: More than a school, it's a family.
testimonials.t4: It's wonderful to be able to share Brazilian culture with my children, and BREACC helps us keep that culture alive in our children who were born outside Brazil.
testimonials.t5: An excellent initiative — this is how we preserve our culture and language! It's gratifying to know that these efforts are being carried out with so much love and dedication!

team.title: Meet the Committee
team.teachers_desc: This section will be updated soon with the profiles of our teachers and assistants.
team.katia_bio: Originally from São Paulo, Brazil, Katia holds a degree in Performing Arts and Art Education. Having lived in London for 23 years, after the birth of her first child she became involved with Portuguese as a Heritage Language with the aim of passing on the culture and Portuguese language to her son. Over the years she has continued to develop her skills by attending courses and conferences. She worked at BREACC as a teacher for over ten years, as events coordinator and teachers' representative. Since 2018 she has been Chairperson/Trustee of BREACC (Brazilian Educational and Cultural Centre), the oldest POLH school in the UK. She has spoken at PLH events in Arizona, Orlando and Dubai, sharing her experience of POLH in the United Kingdom. She is one of the directors of POLH UK, a director of Focus Brasil UK, and carries out art therapy work with the elderly.
team.glayson_bio: Graduated with a full degree in Letters — Portuguese, English and their Literatures — from Unileste MG (University of Eastern Minas Gerais). Portuguese teacher at Kingston University since September 2014. Worked at BREACC from November 2009 to July 2020 as a Portuguese as a Heritage Language teacher for children aged 7 to 13. In January 2018 he became Secretary/Trustee of BREACC. Father of two daughters at BREACC.
team.aracele_bio: Graduated in Foreign Trade Administration with a postgraduate degree in Financial Management from the University of Western Santa Catarina. Studied Accounting and Management at ULSTER University London. Mother of two children at BREACC. Part of the BREACC family since 2019. Started volunteering at BREACC in 2021 as Trustee and Treasurer. Being part of this school has greatly contributed to her children's learning and experience with the Portuguese language, having Portuguese as a heritage language and ensuring that our children learn not only our language but also our culture and customs, making it something precious in their lives.
team.geovanna_bio: BREACC has been part of my life since 2010 and I am a Trustee and part of the committee. I watched my daughter grow up at BREACC and over these years I have had the privilege of making great friendships. I have worked in the aviation industry for 25 years and use my experience in organisation and management as a volunteer.
team.catia_bio: My name is Catia. I graduated in Tourism from the University of São Paulo and hold a Master's in Entrepreneurship and Business Innovation from Bournemouth University. In 2015, some parents and I started the BREACC project in Bournemouth, with the aim of promoting Brazilian culture for our children. This joining of forces has always been the great motivator for the project's continuity, which today has become a reference point in the city and surrounding areas. Organising events, coordinating activities and researching the phenomenon of third culture and heritage language are some of the ways I contribute to the project.
team.michele_bio: Graduated in Journalism from the Metropolitan University of Londrina and entrepreneur running a Transfers and City Tours company. Mother of two children, aged 9 and 6, both learning to read and write through the wonderful BREACC teachers. I often say that BREACC was love at first VISIT. I attended a Festa Junina and loved the atmosphere, the organisation and the unity of the BREACC team. I enrolled my children and volunteered to help with the project. Since then I have been the Administrative Assistant at BREACC for 5 years — years of learning, love, care and lots of patience, where I learn and pass on my experiences.
team.rita_bio:
• Teacher Training College - Primary Teacher at Colégio Lusocarioca/RJ
  • Degree in Pedagogy with specialisations in Teaching and Educational Guidance - UNISUAM/RJ
  • Master's in Education and Training - branch of Language Didactics at the University of Aveiro
  • POLH teacher since 2012 - From 2012 to 2017 at BREACC, 2017 to present at Associação Raízes in Geneva
  • Workshops delivered: Theory of Practice - Florence 2016 and Dubai 2018 / BREACC - Kingston University 2018 / Mixed-level Classes - Associação Raízes (continuing education) 2018 / Authentic Materials - Sorbonne 2019

BREACC and I have a beautiful and long love story. It was where I discovered POLH, where I learnt that we can learn and teach more than just language, where I had the opportunity to become a POLH teacher and now, a new challenge!

history.title: Our History
history.p1: BREACC is a non-profit organisation, created and maintained by volunteer parents who, wishing to see their Brazilian language and culture passed on to their children, came together and organised a small group that has since grown and is now established with centres in Richmond and Bournemouth.
history.p2: As the oldest Portuguese as a Heritage Language school in England, established in 1997, BREACC takes pride in being part of the history of hundreds of families who have contributed to its creation, development and have also benefited from this project.
history.p3: Much more than simply teaching the Portuguese language in a traditional way, our aim is to introduce children to different learning pathways through the diversity of Brazilian culture, involving crafts, music, poetry, literature, history, legends, theatre and food — all in Portuguese. And it is this differentiated teaching and learning process that we call POLH.
history.quote: When we learn a Heritage Language, we are in fact discovering our roots and building our identity.
history.p5: And so, over the years, BREACC has been teaching in Portuguese how to be Brazilian in the land of the King.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about BREACC.
faq.q1: Do children aged 2 and 3 stay alone in the classroom?
faq.a1: No, the Jardim class has active participation from parents.
faq.q2: Do parents have to stay at the school?
faq.a2: Yes, parents should remain at the school and can wait in the canteen.
faq.q3: Do students need to bring a packed lunch?
faq.a3: Yes, the lunch box should be clearly labelled. Important: the school is Nut FREE.
faq.q4: Does the school have a car park?
faq.a4: Yes, but it is important to arrive early to avoid inconvenience.
faq.q5: Does BREACC prepare students for the GCSE?
faq.a5: Yes. BREACC offers teenagers support to prepare for the Portuguese language GCSE exams in a fun and enjoyable way, with reading, speaking and writing mock exams.
faq.a6: Saturdays from 10:30 to 12:30. Our calendar follows the British school calendar.
faq.a7: From two and a half to 15 years old.
faq.q8: What type of organisation is BREACC?
faq.a8: BREACC is a non-profit organisation — a registered Charity in the United Kingdom.
faq.q9: Is it possible to have a trial lesson?
faq.a9: Yes. We offer an open day each term, on Saturdays from 10:30 to 11:30.
faq.q10: Does the child need to speak and understand Portuguese?
faq.a10: It is not a mandatory requirement, although some understanding of the language, however small, is a bonus.
faq.a11: According to the child's age, profile and school year.
faq.q12: When can I enrol my child(ren)?
faq.a12: BREACC accepts enrolments all year round, subject to availability. Please contact us by email: info@breacc.com.
faq.q13: What are the fees and payment methods?
faq.a13: Enrolment: £20 per family. Twickenham: 12× £54.50/month. Bournemouth: 12× £38.50/month. Families with more than one child receive a discount. Payment by direct debit.
